jokes.text: Michael shouting 'Parkour!' repeatedly while attempting parkour in the office
jokes.setup: Michael enthusiastically introducing parkour as an 'internet sensation of 2004'
jokes.punchline: The visual of middle-aged office workers awkwardly attempting extreme sports indoors
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Michael - taking something trendy and completely missing the point. The sustained visual comedy builds well.

jokes.text: As long as point 'a' is delusion and point 'b' is the hospital
jokes.setup: Michael explaining parkour's goal of getting from point A to point B creatively
jokes.punchline: Jim's dry assessment that their only achievement will be injuring themselves
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay and structure. Perfect economy - maximum funny per word. Classic Jim deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Truck to refrigerators to dumpster, 360 spin onto the pallets, backflip gainer into the trash can
jokes.setup: The group planning an elaborate parkour sequence
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous and impossible moves they think they can perform
jokes.explanation: Good escalation but fairly predictable. The absurdity works but isn't particularly original.

jokes.text: Parkour. [single, defeated delivery after presumably failed attempt]
jokes.setup: The elaborate parkour plan they just described
jokes.punchline: Michael's deflated, solo 'parkour' after what was clearly a disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and economy. One word that says everything about how badly it went. Great character beat.

jokes.text: He didn't do anything sexual. He just made far, far too many Monica Lewinsky jokes
jokes.setup: Reference to Michael's 'Monica Lewinsky incident' that ended the intern program
jokes.punchline: The anticlimatic revelation that it was just inappropriate jokes, not actual misconduct
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor. The 'far, far too many' emphasizes Michael's complete lack of restraint.

jokes.text: If they get married before I do, I'm gonna kill myself
jokes.setup: Discussion about intern couple possibly getting married
jokes.punchline: Kelly's dramatic overreaction to being unmarried
jokes.explanation: Typical Kelly dramatics but nothing particularly clever about the structure or delivery.

jokes.text: This place is like Spanish fly
jokes.setup: Michael claiming credit as matchmaker for the intern couple
jokes.punchline: Michael's inappropriate and confused analogy
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate reference but not particularly well-crafted or surprising.

jokes.text: Wait, there's not gonna be a shotgun? No shotgun? 'Cause that changes everything
jokes.setup: Pam mentioning there won't be a shotgun wedding
jokes.punchline: Jim's mock disappointment about the lack of forced marriage drama
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jim's playful side. Nice build-up with the repetition.

jokes.text: Free at last. Free at last
jokes.setup: Joking about backing out of the wedding without a shotgun
jokes.punchline: Jim's mock-dramatic freedom declaration
jokes.explanation: Nice reference and delivery but not particularly original or surprising.

jokes.text: I guess I have a face you can trust. I think it's because of my low cheekbones
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why people tell him things
jokes.punchline: His bizarre theory about facial features determining trustworthiness
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael pseudo-scientific explanation. Good character moment but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Whether it's not being picked for a team or being picked for a team and then showing up and realizing that the team doesn't exist or that the sport doesn't exist. I should have known. Poopball?
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his hatred of being left out
jokes.punchline: The escalating absurdity ending with the revelation he fell for 'Poopball'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with perfect Michael character moment. 'Poopball' is instantly quotable and reveals so much about him.

jokes.text: She said the third wheel is what makes it a tricycle
jokes.setup: Andy trying to comfort Michael about being a third wheel
jokes.punchline: His mother's completely unhelpful but earnest wisdom
jokes.explanation: Great Andy moment - trying to be helpful but offering meaningless platitudes. Good structure and character integration.

jokes.text: Stanley is way past the middle of his life, especially considering his height to weight ratio
jokes.setup: Discussion of Stanley having a midlife crisis
jokes.punchline: Michael's brutal assessment of Stanley's life expectancy based on his physical condition
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's very Michael - inappropriate but delivered with matter-of-fact confidence.

jokes.text: You stupid son of a bitch. You set me up
jokes.setup: Michael realizing Jim fed him false information
jokes.punchline: Michael's disproportionate anger at being 'tricked' into gossip
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but the structure and payoff are fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Drugs? Making out.
jokes.setup: Jim asking what illegal thing Stanley and his 'sister' were doing
jokes.punchline: Michael's innocent assumption followed by the uncomfortable reality
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with excellent economy. The beat between 'drugs?' and the reality is well-timed.

jokes.text: When you have somebody's attention and their eyes are lighting up because they are very interested in what you have to say, that is a great feeling and I experienced that firsthand today. It is wonderful to be the center of attention
jokes.setup: Michael in talking head about spreading gossip
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding that people are interested in the gossip, not him
jokes.explanation: Good character insight but overly verbose. Shows Michael's narcissism well but could be more economical.

jokes.text: I'm casting a movie and I'm looking for a woman who can dance. Beautiful woman. Do you know anybody?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to probe Stanley about his affair indirectly
jokes.punchline: His transparently obvious and inappropriate attempt at subtlety
jokes.explanation: Good Michael moment showing his complete lack of subtlety, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Pays $5 million. Nudity required.
jokes.setup: Michael's fake movie casting call
jokes.punchline: His desperate escalation with increasingly inappropriate details
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation but relies on cheapness (nudity = funny) rather than clever writing.

jokes.text: Time to stop being polite and get real
jokes.setup: Michael deciding to confront Stanley directly
jokes.punchline: His misuse of the Real World catchphrase in completely inappropriate context
jokes.explanation: Good character moment but the reference feels dated and not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Racial profiling gossip
jokes.setup: Michael trying to cover for his false accusation about Stanley
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate attempt to blame racism for the misidentification
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael - making an uncomfortable situation worse with inappropriate explanations. Good cringe value.

jokes.text: We used to go on these long walks on the treadmill
jokes.setup: Stanley explaining his relationship with his physical therapist
jokes.punchline: The inherent contradiction of 'walking' on a stationary treadmill
jokes.explanation: Subtle and well-crafted. The absurdity is delivered matter-of-factly, which makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Do people often say they're going on sales calls and then go someplace else? 'Cause that's not cool
jokes.setup: Learning Stanley has been lying about sales calls
jokes.punchline: Michael's misplaced moral outrage about workplace honesty while ignoring the affair
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. Michael completely misses the point about adultery but is outraged about lying to HR. Perfect priorities.

jokes.text: How do you untell something? You can't. You can't put words back in your mouth
jokes.setup: Michael realizing he's spread Stanley's secret
jokes.punchline: His profound observation about the impossibility of unsaying things
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but not particularly original or insightful.

jokes.text: It's like the end of Spartacus. I have seen that movie half a dozen times and I still don't know who the real Spartacus is. And that is what makes that movie a classic whodunit
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his plan to spread false rumors
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding of the famous 'I am Spartacus' scene as a mystery
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant Michael moment. Completely missing the emotional core of Spartacus and turning it into a detective story. Peak character comedy.

jokes.text: She's an 'anorexitec'
jokes.setup: Michael trying to say 'anorexic'
jokes.punchline: His mangled pronunciation creating a nonsense word
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael mispronunciation but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: Do you like it as much as you like men's butts? 'Cause you're gay
jokes.setup: Andy saying he likes tea
jokes.punchline: Michael's crude and direct confrontation about the gay rumor
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor but relies on cheap shock value rather than clever writing. Very Michael but not well-crafted.

jokes.text: This is not the first time rumors about me being gay have come up. Twice before, actually. Just a weird coincidence. It's a little too weird. Almost makes you wonder if it's not a coincidence at all. Which it is, of course. But it makes you wonder
jokes.setup: Andy addressing the gay rumors
jokes.punchline: His circular logic getting more confused as he talks himself into suspicion
jokes.explanation: Excellent character writing. Andy's tendency to overthink and confuse himself is perfectly captured in the circular structure.

jokes.text: I'm at, like, a beach cabana and Brad Pitt approaches, tries to lean in and kiss me. I would definitely resist, like, at first. But if he was persistent, I might give in a little bit to just really see what it felt like
jokes.setup: Andy trying to prove he's not gay with a hypothetical scenario
jokes.punchline: His 'straight' scenario that sounds increasingly gay as he elaborates
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and character comedy. Andy completely undermining his own point while being totally sincere about it.

jokes.text: What exactly is my responsibility here? To comfort insecure heterosexual men? That can't possibly fall to me
jokes.setup: Andy asking Oscar for reassurance about his sexuality
jokes.punchline: Oscar's exasperated recognition of the absurd position he's been put in
jokes.explanation: Exceptional writing. Perfect economy and Oscar's perspective as the only openly gay person being asked to validate someone's straightness. Brilliant.

jokes.text: Look to the intern on your left. Now to your right. One of you will do exceedingly well in business. Just unlimited potential. One of you will make a living and nothing more. And one of you will make a great mother
jokes.setup: Michael giving a graduation speech to interns
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate assumption that one female intern's highest achievement will be motherhood
jokes.explanation: Good structure building to the sexist punchline. Very Michael to think he's being inspirational while being offensive.

jokes.text: Are you interested in her guy friends?
jokes.setup: Andy asking about Pam's guy friends after Jim suggests her women friends
jokes.punchline: Jim's perfectly logical follow-up that highlights Andy's confusion
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man work from Jim. Economical and logical response that makes Andy's uncertainty more apparent.

jokes.text: You know, a baby conceived out of wedlock is still a bastard
jokes.setup: Jim getting congratulations on the pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Angela's judgmental and archaic moral pronouncement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela moment - completely inappropriate but delivered with total conviction. Great character integration.

jokes.text: If I can't scuba, then what's this all been about? What am I working toward?
jokes.setup: Creed worried about an asthma rumor affecting his scuba diving
jokes.punchline: His existential crisis over scuba diving being the only meaning in his work life
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed moment. The idea that scuba diving is his entire life purpose is perfectly absurd and delivered with total sincerity.

jokes.text: You think I'm the voice of the Taco Bell dog?
jokes.setup: Discussion of various false rumors
jokes.punchline: The completely random and nonsensical rumor about Oscar
jokes.explanation: Random absurdist humor but not particularly well-integrated or meaningful to Oscar's character.

jokes.text: I have a daughter. How could I be a virgin?
jokes.setup: Discussion of false rumors about everyone
jokes.punchline: Darryl's logical refutation of his alleged virginity
jokes.explanation: Good logical humor with nice economy. Simple but effective refutation of an absurd rumor.

jokes.text: Who's been saying that there's another person inside of me working me with controls?
jokes.setup: Kevin asking about rumors about himself
jokes.punchline: The specific absurd rumor about him being controlled like a robot
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kevin rumor - absurd but oddly plausible given his behavior. Very creative and memorable.

jokes.text: You told people that I use store-bought manure when I showed you where my manure comes from
jokes.setup: Dwight confronting Michael about false rumors
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Dwight has shown Michael his manure source and considers this a betrayal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Dwight. The idea that he's given Michael a personal tour of his manure source is absurd and character-perfect.

jokes.text: I spread the rumor that somebody here was a J.Crew model and I was referring to myself
jokes.setup: Michael defending his rumor-spreading
jokes.punchline: His narcissistic assumption that he would be mistaken for a model
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's delusions of grandeur, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Her breasts were a tiny bit bigger. At first I thought, oh, she has a new bra with padding. But then I thought, Pam doesn't need padding
jokes.setup: Kevin explaining how he knew Pam was pregnant
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate detailed observation and creepy conclusion
jokes.explanation: Pure Kevin inappropriate observation but relies heavily on cringe without much clever writing.

jokes.text: That is the inside of your vagina
jokes.setup: Looking at Pam's ultrasound
jokes.punchline: Michael's completely wrong and inappropriate interpretation of ultrasound technology
jokes.explanation: Very Michael to misunderstand medical technology and say something inappropriate, but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: You should have told me. You're right. We should have realized that you are an equal part of this.
jokes.setup: Michael saying they should have told him about pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Pam's sarcastic agreement to his absurd claim of involvement
jokes.explanation: Good Pam sarcasm in response to Michael's self-centered reaction, solid structure.

jokes.text: We should have realized that you are an equal part of this
jokes.setup: Michael complaining they didn't tell him about the pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Pam's perfectly sarcastic acknowledgment of Michael's 'contribution' to their pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Excellent sarcasm from Pam. Perfect economy and delivery that shuts down Michael's narcissistic complaint.

jokes.text: Hey, what up, Cynthia?
jokes.setup: Michael answering a call from Stanley's wife Teri
jokes.punchline: His immediate mistake of calling Stanley's wife by his mistress's name
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff. Michael immediately making the worst possible mistake demonstrates his complete inability to handle delicate situations.

jokes.text: So you can't stop love, I guess
jokes.setup: Michael explaining the complicated affair situation
jokes.punchline: His romantic rationalization of adultery
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment but fairly predictable Michael romanticizing of inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: I thought it was 500. Oh. I guess I didn't learn anything
jokes.setup: One intern saying they learned a ream is 300 sheets
jokes.punchline: Second intern's realization they were wrong and learned nothing
jokes.explanation: Nice self-deprecating beat with good economy. Simple but effective ending moment.

jokes.text: I learned that if you look even the tiniest bit like Jet Li, Michael will call you Jet Li all summer. Julia Stiles. Alan Thicke
jokes.setup: Interns sharing what they learned about Michael
jokes.punchline: The revelation of Michael's celebrity name-calling pattern and increasingly random comparisons
jokes.explanation: Good character insight about Michael's bizarre behavior patterns. The escalation from Jet Li to Alan Thicke is nice.
